title: muehlenbeckia adpressa var. flexuosa
intro: Muehlenbeckia adpressa var. flexuosa, also known as the climbing lilyvine or flexible lilyvine, is a climbing plant in the family Polygonaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. The health benefits of Muehlenbeckia adpressa var. flexuosa include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Muehlenbeckia adpressa var. flexuosa is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
